wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the DML Language Server project overview. Explain the purpose of providing IDE support for Intel Simics Device Modeling Language (DML), its core capabilities including syntax analysis, semantic analysis, symbol resolution, and configurable linting. Document the project's architecture as a Language Server Protocol implementation, target audience (IDE developers and DML users), and relationship to the broader Simics ecosystem. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners new to DML and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase such as 'DML 1.4', 'LSP', 'symbol resolution', and 'virtual file system'. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases like IDE integration and command-line analysis. Document the project's scope, limitations (DML 1.4 only), and future roadmap.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the DML Language Server system overview. Explain the overall architecture, high-level design principles, and system boundaries. Document the main components including the Language Server Protocol implementation, analysis engine, virtual file system, and action coordination system. Describe how these components work together to provide IDE support for DML device modeling. Include system context diagrams showing the relationship between the language server, IDE clients, and external tools. Explain the event-driven architecture pattern and how asynchronous processing enables efficient handling of multiple concurrent requests. Address the modular design philosophy and how different subsystems can be understood and maintained independently.

wiki_catalogs.name: Parsing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the DML parsing system. Explain the lexical analysis phase with DML-specific tokenization rules, custom lexer implementation using Logos crate, and token classification including keywords, operators, literals, and identifiers. Document the recursive descent parsing algorithm with lookahead capabilities, syntax tree construction, and error recovery mechanisms. Detail the AST node types, tree element hierarchy, and position tracking systems. Include concrete examples showing token streams, parse tree construction, and error handling scenarios. Document the relationship between lexical and syntactic phases, performance optimizations for large files, and extensibility points for adding new language constructs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lint Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the lint configuration system. Explain the LintCfg struct and its JSON configuration format, including all available rule categories (sp_reserved, sp_brace, sp_punct, sp_binop, sp_ternary, sp_ptrdecl, nsp_ptrdecl, nsp_funpar, nsp_inparen, nsp_unary, nsp_trailing, long_lines, indent_size, indent_no_tabs, indent_code_block, indent_closing_brace, indent_paren_expr, indent_switch_case, indent_empty_loop) with their default values and parameter options. Document the configuration file parsing mechanism, unknown field detection and warnings, and the annotate_lints flag functionality. Include practical examples of common configuration scenarios, inheritance patterns, and override strategies. Detail the setup_indentation_size function and its role in configuration initialization. Address configuration validation, error handling during parsing, and integration with the broader analysis pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: MCP Server Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the MCP server architecture. Explain the DMLMCPServer implementation, server capabilities configuration, and protocol version management. Document the server initialization process, capability negotiation with clients, and the relationship between MCP and the main DML language server. Detail the server info structure, capability flags (tools, resources, prompts, logging), and how the MCP server integrates with the existing analysis engine. Include practical examples of server configuration, client connection handling, and protocol specifications. Address server lifecycle management, error handling, and performance considerations for concurrent MCP operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message Handling and Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the message handling and communication layer of the DML Language Server. Document the JSON-RPC over stdio communication model, including message parsing, validation, and serialization processes. Explain the RawMessage and RawResponse structures, message reader/writer implementations, and the threading model for client communication. Detail the message lifecycle from reception to dispatch, error handling strategies for malformed messages, and protocol compliance verification. Include practical examples of message exchange patterns, performance considerations for high-throughput scenarios, and debugging techniques for communication issues. Address message buffering, flow control, and resource management in multi-threaded environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: DLS Binary
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the main DLS (Device Language Server) binary. Document the command-line interface including all supported commands (def, symbol, document, open, workspace, context-mode, contexts, set-contexts, wait, help, quit). Explain the interactive mode functionality with step-by-step usage examples, parameter specifications for each command, and expected output formats. Detail the initialization process, workspace management, and device context configuration options. Include practical examples of common workflows like opening files, navigating definitions, searching symbols, and managing workspace folders. Address performance considerations for large projects, memory usage patterns, and debugging techniques. Document the relationship between CLI commands and underlying LSP operations, error handling strategies, and integration with external tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Language Server Protocol API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Language Server Protocol implementation. Document all LSP 3.0 endpoints including initialize, shutdown, textDocument/* requests, and window/* notifications. Detail request/response schemas with parameter validation rules, error codes, and response formats. Include client capability negotiation, dynamic registration mechanisms, and custom protocol extensions. Provide concrete examples of LSP message exchanges, error handling patterns, and performance optimization techniques. Document the message dispatch system, IO handling over stdio, and integration with the analysis engine. Address protocol compliance verification, debugging tools, and common implementation pitfalls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Infrastructure and Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the testing infrastructure and setup procedures. Explain the overall test architecture including the Rust-based test framework, Python port testing infrastructure, and cross-platform compatibility testing strategies. Document the test environment configuration, dependency management, and test execution workflows. Detail the helper functions and utilities provided for testing (make_ast, make_token, test_ast_tree, etc.) and their usage patterns. Include practical examples of setting up test environments, running individual test suites, and interpreting test results. Address the relationship between different test categories and their execution priorities in CI/CD pipelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Action Context Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the action context management system. Explain the ActionContext enum that maintains server state across concurrent operations, including the InitActionContext and UninitActionContext variants. Document the context initialization process, state transitions, and how the context coordinates between different subsystems like analysis storage, virtual file system, and configuration management. Detail the persistent state management including workspace roots, direct open files, compilation info, device active contexts, and job coordination. Include concrete examples of context lifecycle management, thread-safe access patterns using Arc and Mutex, and the relationship between context state and server capabilities. Address debugging approaches for context-related issues and performance considerations for high-concurrency scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Basic Usage Examples
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive basic usage examples demonstrating fundamental DML language server functionality. Include step-by-step setup instructions for IDE integration with popular editors (VS Code, Vim, Emacs). Document command-line analysis examples using the dls binary, including basic linting commands and output interpretation. Show practical examples of DML syntax highlighting, hover information, and basic navigation features. Demonstrate simple lint configuration setup using the provided example_lint_cfg.json. Include real DML code examples from watchdog_timer.dml showing common patterns like device definitions, properties, and methods. Provide troubleshooting guidance for first-time users encountering common setup issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Expression Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for DML expression analysis within the semantic analysis phase. Detail the semantic representation of DML expressions including arithmetic, logical, comparison, and function call expressions. Document the expression type inference algorithms, operator precedence handling, and expression evaluation contexts. Explain how AST expressions are transformed into semantic expression nodes with type information. Include concrete examples of expression analysis scenarios, type checking for expressions, and error handling for malformed expressions. Document the relationship between parsed expressions and semantic types, variable resolution within expressions, and constant folding optimizations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Methods
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for template method handling in the DML template processing system. Explain the method resolution algorithms, parameter binding for templated methods, and inheritance mechanisms for template methods. Document the method signature validation, overload resolution, and virtual method dispatch for templated constructs. Include concrete examples showing method instantiation workflows, parameter substitution, and method resolution order. Detail the relationship between template methods and regular methods, performance considerations for method resolution, and debugging techniques for template method issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lexical Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the lexical analysis phase of the DML parser. Document the custom lexer implementation built on top of the Logos crate, explaining tokenization rules specific to DML language constructs. Detail the token classification system including keywords, operators, literals (strings, numbers, booleans), identifiers, and special symbols. Explain the lexer state machine, multi-character operator handling, string literal processing with escape sequences, and numeric literal recognition. Include examples of token streams for various DML constructs, error handling for invalid characters, and performance optimizations for large input files. Document the integration with the parsing phase and how tokens flow into the syntax analyzer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the DML Language Server. Include detailed installation instructions for building from source using cargo, running pre-built binaries, and setting up the Python port alternative. Document the basic workflow from installation to first successful IDE integration. Explain prerequisite knowledge including DML language basics, LSP protocol understanding, and editor configuration. Provide step-by-step setup instructions for common IDEs, configuration of compile_commands.json, and initial testing procedures. Include troubleshooting for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Address both Rust-based and Python-based deployment scenarios with clear guidance on when to use each approach.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for component interactions within the DML Language Server architecture. Document the communication patterns between major components including the server core, action coordination system, analysis engine, and virtual file system. Explain how requests flow through the system from initial LSP message reception to final response generation. Detail the data exchange mechanisms, shared state management, and synchronization points between components. Include sequence diagrams showing typical request-response cycles and error handling scenarios. Address the role of the action context in coordinating analysis tasks and managing component lifecycles. Document the observer pattern implementation for real-time notifications and change propagation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Semantic Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the semantic analysis phase of the DML analysis engine. Explain the semantic structure extraction from AST nodes, symbol table construction, type checking algorithms, and scope resolution mechanisms. Document the semantic representation of DML constructs including expressions, statements, object declarations, and type definitions. Detail the type system implementation, type inference rules, and type compatibility checking. Include concrete examples showing AST-to-semantic transformation, symbol table operations, and type checking scenarios. Document the relationship between parsed syntax and semantic structures, error reporting during semantic analysis, and performance considerations for large codebases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lint Rules Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the lint rule architecture and classification system. Explain the Rule trait implementation, rule categorization by type (spacing rules, indentation rules, common pattern rules), and the instantiate_rules function for rule instantiation. Document the RuleType enumeration and its mapping to specific rule implementations. Detail the rule execution pipeline, parameter passing through AuxParams, and the relationship between rules and the AST traversal. Include the rule registration mechanism, rule type system, and how rules integrate with the analysis engine. Address rule development patterns, testing methodologies, and performance considerations for rule execution across large codebases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tool Registry System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the MCP tool registry system. Explain the tool registration mechanism, tool metadata management, and the interface for extending the tool collection. Document the available DML-specific tools for code generation, analysis utilities, and template instantiation. Detail the tool execution model, parameter handling, and result processing. Include examples of tool implementation patterns, tool discovery mechanisms, and how tools interact with the analysis engine. Address tool lifecycle management, error propagation, and best practices for developing custom MCP tools that integrate with DML analysis capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Request Dispatch and Routing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the request dispatch and routing system. Explain the Dispatcher architecture, method matching algorithms, and request categorization into notifications, blocking requests, and regular requests. Document the dispatch_action macro system, request/response handling patterns, and the three-tier processing model (immediate, queued, timeout). Detail the RequestAction trait implementation, response serialization, and error propagation mechanisms. Include examples of custom request handlers, performance optimization techniques for request processing, and debugging approaches for dispatch failures. Address concurrent request handling, priority queuing, and resource contention management.

wiki_catalogs.name: DFA (Device File Analyzer)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the DFA (Device File Analyzer) tool. Explain the standalone analysis capabilities for DML device files, including dependency tracking, device graph analysis, and report generation. Document command-line options, input file formats, and output formats. Detail the analysis algorithms used for device file processing, including template instantiation, inheritance resolution, and cross-reference analysis. Include practical examples of common analysis scenarios, batch processing workflows, and integration with build systems. Address performance optimization for large device hierarchies, memory management considerations, and debugging analysis failures. Document the relationship between DFA and the main language server, shared configuration options, and migration paths for users transitioning from manual analysis to automated workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Context Protocol API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Model Context Protocol implementation. Document MCP server endpoints, tool registration mechanisms, and template-based code generation APIs. Detail the tool interface specification, parameter schemas, and response formats for AI-assisted development features. Include MCP message protocols, authentication requirements, and session management. Provide examples of tool implementation, template instantiation patterns, and integration with the analysis engine. Document the MCP server lifecycle, client connections, and error handling strategies. Address performance considerations for AI-assisted workflows and debugging techniques for MCP integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rust Unit Tests
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Rust unit testing strategies and implementation. Explain the unit test architecture focusing on core components like parsing tests, analysis engine tests, and MCP unit tests. Document the testing patterns used for lexical analysis, syntax parsing, semantic analysis, and template processing. Include practical examples of writing unit tests for language server components, mocking external dependencies, and testing error conditions. Detail the test utilities for AST construction, token verification, and parse context testing. Address performance testing approaches, memory safety validation, and concurrent testing strategies for multi-threaded components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Request Processing Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the request processing pipeline that handles LSP requests and responses. Explain the request dispatching mechanism, message parsing and validation, and response generation workflows. Document the HandleResponseType system for managing different response types, request queuing and prioritization, and error propagation through the pipeline. Detail the integration with the server's message handling infrastructure, request validation against client capabilities, and the coordination between synchronous and asynchronous request processing. Include concrete examples of request lifecycle from reception to completion, timeout handling, and cancellation mechanisms. Address performance optimization techniques, memory management for request processing, and debugging approaches for request-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Configuration Examples
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed advanced configuration examples for complex DML projects and specialized use cases. Document comprehensive lint rule configuration with custom parameters, rule disabling/enabling, and per-file overrides. Show multi-device analysis setup for large-scale DML projects with device interdependencies. Demonstrate MCP server configuration for AI-assisted development workflows. Include advanced VFS configuration for handling large codebases and remote file systems. Provide examples of custom analysis tool integration and extended lint rule development. Document performance optimization configurations for large projects including parallel processing settings and memory management options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Statement Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for DML statement processing in the semantic analysis phase. Explain the semantic analysis of control flow statements (if/else, loops, switches), declaration statements, and compound statements. Document statement scoping rules, variable lifetime analysis, and control flow graph construction. Detail the semantic validation of statement sequences, break/continue statement resolution, and return statement type checking. Include examples of statement analysis workflows, scope management during statement processing, and error detection for unreachable code and invalid statement combinations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Objects
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for template object instantiation and management. Explain the object hierarchy construction for templated objects, parameter binding during instantiation, and object lifecycle management. Document the template object creation algorithms, inheritance chains for templated classes, and object validation processes. Include concrete examples showing template object instantiation workflows, parameter resolution, and object hierarchy building. Detail the relationship between template objects and regular objects, memory management considerations, and debugging techniques for template object issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Syntax Parsing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the recursive descent parsing algorithm used in the DML parser. Explain the grammar rules, lookahead mechanisms, and parsing strategy for DML language constructs. Document the parser state management, error recovery techniques, and how the parser handles ambiguous grammars. Detail the integration with the lexer for token consumption, parser method organization, and recursive descent patterns. Include examples of parsing different DML constructs, error scenarios and recovery strategies, and performance considerations for complex syntax trees. Address the relationship between parsing phases and subsequent semantic analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture and Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the DML Language Server system. Describe the high-level design patterns including the Language Server Protocol implementation, modular architecture with clear separation of concerns, and event-driven processing model. Document component interactions between the server core, analysis engine, virtual file system, and action coordination systems. Explain technical decisions such as the choice of Rust for performance-critical analysis, asynchronous processing with tokio, and the layered approach to parsing and semantic analysis. Include system boundaries, data flows from input processing through analysis to output generation, and integration patterns with IDE clients. Address cross-cutting concerns like error handling, logging, and performance optimization. Provide system context diagrams showing relationships between major components and their responsibilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow and Processing Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the data flow and processing pipeline in the DML Language Server. Document the complete analysis pipeline from raw file content through lexical analysis, syntax parsing, semantic analysis, and template processing to final output generation. Explain the layered approach where parsing builds syntax trees, structure extraction creates symbol tables, and templating resolves device hierarchies. Detail the incremental analysis approach and how changes propagate through the system. Include flow diagrams showing data transformation stages and intermediate representations. Address caching strategies, performance optimizations, and memory management throughout the pipeline. Document error handling and recovery mechanisms at each processing stage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Symbol Resolution System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the symbol resolution and scoping system. Explain the symbol table architecture, context-aware symbol lookup algorithms, and scope chain management. Document the symbol types, symbol containers, and symbol lifecycle management. Detail the provisional analysis system for handling forward references, circular dependency detection, and deferred resolution. Include concrete examples showing symbol table operations, scope traversal, and reference resolution scenarios. Document the relationship between symbols and AST nodes, performance optimizations for large symbol tables, and extensibility points for adding new symbol types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Spacing Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the spacing-related lint rules that enforce whitespace and spacing consistency in DML code. Document each spacing rule including SpReservedOptions (reserved keyword spacing), SpBraceOptions (brace spacing), SpPunctOptions (punctuation spacing), SpBinopOptions (binary operator spacing), SpTernaryOptions (ternary operator spacing), SpPtrDeclOptions (pointer declaration spacing), NspPtrDeclOptions (no-space pointer declaration), NspFunparOptions (no-space function parameters), NspInparenOptions (no-space in parentheses), NspUnaryOptions (no-space unary operators), and NspTrailingOptions (no-space trailing). Explain rule parameters, validation logic, error reporting mechanisms, and integration with the broader linting system. Include practical examples of violations and their corrections, performance implications, and testing strategies for spacing rule validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the MCP template system. Explain template definition, instantiation mechanisms, and parameter binding for DML code generation. Document the template syntax, variable substitution, and conditional logic support. Detail how templates leverage the analysis engine for context-aware generation, template validation, and error handling. Include practical examples of template creation, parameter configuration, and generated code patterns. Address template inheritance, reusability patterns, and integration with the broader MCP ecosystem. Document best practices for template design and performance optimization for large-scale code generation tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Client Capability Negotiation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for client capability negotiation and dynamic feature registration. Explain the ServerCapabilities structure, capability advertisement mechanisms, and client capability discovery processes. Document the experimental features system, workspace folder support, and text document synchronization options. Detail the initialization handshake, capability validation, and fallback mechanisms for unsupported features. Include examples of capability queries, dynamic capability updates, and client-specific feature adaptations. Address backward compatibility considerations, capability inheritance patterns, and troubleshooting capability-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: MCP Server
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the MCP (Model Context Protocol) server command-line interface. Document server startup options, port configuration, and network binding parameters. Explain the AI-assisted development workflows enabled by MCP, including tool registration, template-based code generation, and integration with AI assistants. Detail the command-line arguments for server configuration, logging options, and security considerations. Include practical examples of setting up MCP servers, connecting AI assistants, and implementing custom MCP tools. Address performance tuning for production deployments, resource management, and monitoring server health. Document the relationship between MCP server and the main language server, shared analysis capabilities, and migration paths for users adopting AI-assisted development workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the configuration management API. Document all configuration options including LSP initialization parameters, runtime configuration updates, and command-line interface options. Detail the configuration schema, validation rules, and default values. Include examples of configuration files, environment variable overrides, and programmatic configuration access. Document deprecated options handling, configuration migration procedures, and validation error reporting. Provide guidance on configuration best practices, performance tuning options, and troubleshooting configuration issues. Address configuration precedence rules and dynamic reconfiguration capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Python Port Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Python port testing strategies and compatibility validation. Explain the testing approach for ensuring compatibility between the Rust and Python implementations, including functional equivalence testing and API compatibility validation. Document the test suite structure covering basic functionality tests, CLI functionality tests, LSP component tests, advanced LSP feature tests, and error handling tests. Include practical examples of cross-language test validation, performance comparison testing, and integration testing between Python port components. Address the challenges of maintaining test parity across different language implementations and strategies for identifying compatibility issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Notification System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the notification system that handles real-time updates and progress reporting. Explain the notification dispatch mechanism, progress tracking for analysis operations, and diagnostic publishing workflows. Document the different types of notifications including progress updates, diagnostic reports, and workspace change notifications. Detail the integration with LSP protocol notifications, real-time file change propagation, and the coordination between analysis progress and client updates. Include concrete examples of notification lifecycle, batching strategies for multiple notifications, and the relationship between notifications and the broader server architecture. Address performance considerations for high-frequency notifications, memory management for notification queues, and debugging approaches for notification delivery issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed integration pattern documentation showing how to integrate DML language server with various development environments and workflows. Document IDE plugin integration patterns for VS Code, Vim, Emacs, and other editors with specific configuration examples. Show build system integration with CMake, Make, and other project builders including compile_commands.json configuration. Demonstrate CI/CD pipeline integration for automated DML analysis and linting in continuous integration workflows. Include Docker containerization examples for consistent development environments. Provide examples of custom client development and protocol extension patterns. Document integration with version control systems and pre-commit hooks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Object Structure Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for DML object structure analysis within the semantic phase. Explain the semantic representation of DML objects including class definitions, interface declarations, and object hierarchies. Document object member analysis, inheritance resolution, and polymorphism handling. Detail the semantic validation of object definitions, member visibility rules, and object lifecycle management. Include examples of object structure analysis, type compatibility checking for object hierarchies, and error reporting for invalid object definitions. Document the relationship between parsed object declarations and semantic object models.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Topology
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for template topology analysis and device network construction. Explain the topology resolution algorithms, device interconnection analysis, and hierarchical topology building for templated systems. Document the topology validation processes, dependency resolution, and circular dependency detection for template hierarchies. Include concrete examples showing topology instantiation workflows, device connection analysis, and topology validation scenarios. Detail the relationship between template topology and device networks, performance considerations for large topologies, and debugging techniques for topology-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Abstract Syntax Tree
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) construction and management system. Document the AST node types, tree element hierarchy, and position tracking mechanisms using ZeroSpan and ZeroRange structures. Explain the tree construction process during parsing, node relationships, and how the AST preserves source location information. Detail the tree traversal patterns, visitor implementations, and integration with semantic analysis. Include examples of AST representations for various DML constructs, memory management strategies, and performance optimizations for large syntax trees. Address tree manipulation operations and transformation utilities used by later analysis phases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Language Server Protocol Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Language Server Protocol implementation. Document LSP 3.0 specification compliance, request/response handling mechanisms, and client capability negotiation processes. Explain the JSON-RPC over stdio communication model, message parsing and validation, and the dispatch system for routing requests to appropriate handlers. Detail supported LSP features including textDocument/definition, textDocument/references, textDocument/hover, and workspace/symbol requests. Document custom protocol extensions specific to DML analysis, initialization options, and dynamic capability registration. Include practical examples of LSP message exchanges, error handling strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Address client implementation guidelines for IDE developers and debugging approaches for protocol-level issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design Patterns and Architectural Principles
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the design patterns and architectural principles underlying the DML Language Server. Document the use of actor model patterns for event-driven processing, strategy pattern for pluggable analysis approaches, and observer pattern for change notifications. Explain the layered architecture principle and how it enables separation of concerns between parsing, analysis, and presentation layers. Detail the factory pattern implementation for dynamic creation of analysis contexts and the plugin architecture for extensible linting rules. Address concurrency patterns including thread pools, async processing with tokio, and safe shared state management. Include examples from the codebase showing how these patterns are implemented and their benefits for system maintainability and performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reference Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the cross-document reference tracking system. Explain the reference model, global reference resolution, and inter-file symbol relationships. Document the reference matching algorithms, reference cache mechanisms, and incremental update strategies. Detail the handling of forward references, circular dependencies, and reference validation. Include concrete examples showing reference resolution workflows, cache operations, and error reporting for unresolved references. Document the integration with symbol resolution, performance optimizations for large projects, and debugging techniques for reference-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Indentation Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the indentation-related lint rules that enforce consistent indentation practices in DML code. Document each indentation rule including LongLineOptions (line length limits), IndentSizeOptions (indentation size configuration), IndentNoTabOptions (tab prohibition), IndentCodeBlockOptions (code block indentation), IndentClosingBraceOptions (closing brace indentation), IndentParenExprOptions (parentheses expression indentation), IndentSwitchCaseOptions (switch case indentation), and IndentEmptyLoopOptions (empty loop indentation). Explain the depth parameter system for nested structure handling, indentation calculation algorithms, and hierarchical indentation enforcement. Detail the post-processing logic that removes redundant errors, integration with AST traversal, and performance optimizations for large indentation checks. Include examples of complex indentation scenarios, edge cases, and debugging techniques for indentation rule violations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Code Generation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the MCP code generation engine. Explain the generation pipeline, from template selection to final code output. Document the integration with the analysis engine for context-aware generation, symbol resolution during generation, and validation of generated code. Detail the generation strategies, output formatting, and error recovery mechanisms. Include examples of generation scenarios, parameter tuning, and quality assurance for generated code. Address performance considerations for batch generation, memory management, and scalability aspects. Document the relationship between generation results and the virtual file system for seamless integration with IDE workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Protocol Extensions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for DML-specific protocol extensions and custom message types. Document the experimental features implementation, custom notification types, and extended request handlers. Explain the experimental capabilities structure, custom initialization options, and DML-specific analysis commands. Detail the integration points for device context management, custom completion providers, and specialized diagnostic reporting. Include examples of custom protocol implementations, extension registration patterns, and client-side extension handling. Address versioning strategies for custom extensions, compatibility maintenance, and migration paths for protocol evolution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Internal Extension API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for internal APIs designed for extension development and custom analysis tools. Document the analysis engine interfaces, action context APIs, and utility functions available for plugin development. Detail the extension point architecture, hook registration mechanisms, and custom analysis integration patterns. Include examples of extending the parser, adding custom linting rules, and integrating external analysis tools. Document thread safety considerations, memory management patterns, and performance optimization guidelines for extensions. Address API stability guarantees, version compatibility, and migration strategies for extension developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration and End-to-End Tests
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for integration and end-to-end testing strategies. Explain the testing approach for validating complete system workflows including installation testing, MCP integration testing, and full language server functionality validation. Document the test scenarios covering multi-file analysis, LSP protocol compliance testing, and real-world usage patterns. Include practical examples of testing the entire analysis pipeline from file input to LSP responses, validating MCP tool integration, and testing cross-document references. Address the setup of test environments that mimic production deployments, testing of external tool integrations, and validation of system-level performance and reliability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Work Pool Coordination
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the work pool coordination system that manages parallel analysis tasks and resource allocation. Explain the concurrent job management, work queue prioritization, and resource throttling mechanisms. Document the Jobs and ConcurrentJob systems, work pool sizing strategies, and load balancing across analysis operations. Detail the integration with the analysis queue, progress tracking for queued operations, and cancellation mechanisms for long-running tasks. Include concrete examples of work pool configuration, job scheduling algorithms, and the relationship between work pool capacity and server performance. Address performance optimization techniques, deadlock prevention strategies, and debugging approaches for concurrent operation issues. Document the coordination between work pool and other server components for optimal resource utilization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Best Practices and Workflows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive best practices documentation covering optimal DML development workflows and coding standards. Document recommended project structure patterns for DML codebases including file organization, naming conventions, and modular design principles. Show efficient development workflows integrating language server features like incremental analysis, symbol navigation, and refactoring support. Provide guidelines for team collaboration including shared lint configurations, code review processes, and consistency enforcement. Include performance optimization techniques for large DML projects such as selective analysis, caching strategies, and resource management. Document debugging workflows using language server diagnostics and error reporting. Address maintenance best practices including code cleanup, dependency management, and upgrade strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Type System and Checking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the DML type system implementation within semantic analysis. Detail the type hierarchy, primitive types, composite types, and generic type handling. Document type inference algorithms, type compatibility rules, and type coercion mechanisms. Explain type checking for expressions, assignments, and function calls. Include examples of type inference scenarios, type compatibility validation, and error reporting for type mismatches. Document the relationship between type definitions and semantic type representations, type parameter resolution, and type safety guarantees.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Traits
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for template trait resolution and implementation analysis. Explain the trait matching algorithms, template constraint resolution, and trait inheritance mechanisms for templated constructs. Document the trait validation processes, conflict resolution, and trait specialization handling. Include concrete examples showing trait resolution workflows, constraint satisfaction, and trait implementation verification. Detail the relationship between template traits and regular traits, performance considerations for trait resolution, and debugging techniques for template trait issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Expression Parsing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for expression parsing capabilities within the DML parser. Document the expression grammar rules, operator precedence handling, and associativity resolution. Explain support for various expression types including arithmetic, logical, comparison, and function call expressions. Detail the recursive parsing approach for nested expressions, parenthesized expressions, and unary/binary operators. Include examples of expression AST construction, precedence evaluation, and error handling for malformed expressions. Address the integration with type checking and semantic analysis phases that rely on properly parsed expressions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analysis Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the DML analysis engine subsystem. Explain the multi-stage parsing architecture including lexical analysis with DML-specific tokenization, recursive descent parsing with error recovery, and syntax tree construction. Document the semantic analysis pipeline covering symbol table construction, type checking, scope resolution, and cross-document reference tracking. Detail the symbol resolution system with context-aware lookups, reference tracking across multiple files, and provisional analysis for forward references. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing AST traversal, symbol table operations, and error reporting mechanisms. Document the relationship between parsing and semantic phases, performance considerations for large codebases, and extensibility points for adding new analysis features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the DML template processing system. Explain the template instantiation algorithms, object hierarchy construction, and template trait resolution. Document the template method handling, topology analysis, and type resolution for templated constructs. Detail the template object creation, parameter binding, and inheritance mechanisms. Include concrete examples showing template instantiation workflows, object hierarchy building, and template validation scenarios. Document the relationship between templates and regular objects, performance considerations for complex template hierarchies, and debugging techniques for template-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lint Annotations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the per-file and per-line lint annotation system that allows selective rule disabling. Explain the dml-lint annotation syntax, supported operations (allow, allow-file), rule targeting mechanisms, and annotation parsing logic. Document the LintAnnotation enum, LintAnnotations struct with line-specific and whole-file annotation storage, and the annotation application process. Detail the regular expression patterns for annotation detection, annotation stacking behavior, and error reporting for invalid annotations. Include practical examples of annotation usage scenarios, best practices for selective linting, and integration with the linting pipeline. Address annotation precedence rules, performance considerations for annotation processing, and debugging techniques for annotation-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top-level Structures
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for top-level DML structure analysis including global declarations, namespace handling, and module-level constructs. Explain the semantic analysis of global variables, functions, and import statements. Document namespace resolution, symbol visibility across files, and module dependency management. Detail the semantic validation of top-level declarations, global scope management, and cross-file symbol resolution. Include examples of top-level structure analysis, namespace collision handling, and import resolution algorithms. Document the integration between top-level analysis and the overall symbol table construction.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Types
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for template type resolution and type system integration. Explain the type instantiation algorithms, generic type parameter binding, and type constraint validation for templated constructs. Document the type resolution processes, type compatibility checking, and type inference for template parameters. Include concrete examples showing type instantiation workflows, parameter resolution, and type validation scenarios. Detail the relationship between template types and the broader type system, performance considerations for type resolution, and debugging techniques for template type issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Statement Parsing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for statement parsing in the DML language. Document the supported statement types including variable declarations, assignments, control flow statements (if/else, loops), function calls, and compound statements. Explain the parsing logic for each statement category, block handling, and statement sequencing. Detail the integration with expression parsing for statement operands, scope handling, and statement validation rules. Include examples of statement AST construction, error recovery for malformed statements, and the relationship between statement parsing and subsequent semantic analysis. Address special DML statement constructs and their unique parsing requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Virtual File System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Virtual File System implementation. Explain the file caching strategy, change tracking mechanisms, and synchronization between in-memory representations and disk files. Document the multi-threaded file operations with proper locking mechanisms, file watching for external changes, and integration with the analysis engine for automatic re-analysis. Detail the file management APIs including read operations, write buffering, and cache invalidation strategies. Include examples of file change propagation through the system, performance optimizations for large projects, and memory management considerations. Address edge cases like concurrent file modifications, network file systems, and handling of very large DML files. Document the relationship between VFS and the broader analysis pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lint Testing Framework
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the lint testing framework and unit testing strategies. Explain the test infrastructure setup, ExpectedDMLStyleError structure for test assertions, and the assert_snippet testing helper function. Document the test organization by rule categories, test case creation patterns, and assertion mechanisms for lint rule validation. Detail the integration with the broader test suite, test data management, and continuous integration testing. Include guidelines for writing effective lint rule tests, debugging test failures, and maintaining test coverage. Address performance testing considerations, edge case testing strategies, and best practices for ensuring lint rule reliability across different code patterns and scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Structure Parsing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for DML structure parsing including object definitions, class hierarchies, and module structures. Document the parsing of DML top-level constructs, object declarations, inheritance relationships, and structural elements. Explain the parsing of interface definitions, trait implementations, and structural type checking. Detail the integration with the overall parsing pipeline, validation rules for structural constructs, and error handling for malformed structures. Include examples of structure AST construction, parsing of complex object hierarchies, and the relationship between structure parsing and semantic analysis for type checking and symbol resolution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Linting System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the configurable linting system. Explain the style checking architecture with pluggable rule system, rule configuration management, and per-file/per-line annotation capabilities. Document the available linting rules organized by categories (indentation, spacing, common patterns), rule parameters and customization options, and integration with the analysis engine for real-time feedback. Detail the lint configuration file format, inheritance mechanisms, and override strategies. Include practical examples of common linting scenarios, rule selection guidelines, and performance considerations for large codebases. Address rule development for extending the linting capabilities, debugging lint violations, and balancing strictness with developer productivity. Document the relationship between linting and the broader analysis pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Type System Parsing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for type system parsing within the DML language. Document the parsing of type declarations, type modifiers, generic type parameters, and type constraints. Explain the integration of type information with expression and statement parsing, type annotation handling, and type inference support. Detail the parsing of function signatures, parameter types, return types, and exception types. Include examples of type AST construction, type validation rules, and the relationship between type parsing and semantic analysis for type checking and compatibility verification.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Context Protocol
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Model Context Protocol implementation. Explain the AI-assisted development features including the MCP server architecture, tool registry system, and template-based code generation capabilities. Document the available MCP tools for DML development, template instantiation mechanisms, and integration with the analysis engine for context-aware assistance. Detail the MCP server configuration, client connection handling, and protocol specifications for AI assistants. Include practical examples of MCP tool usage, template customization, and extending the tool registry with custom analysis utilities. Address the relationship between MCP and traditional LSP features, performance considerations for AI-assisted operations, and security implications of AI integration. Document best practices for leveraging MCP features in development workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Miscellaneous Parsing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for auxiliary parsing functions and utilities that support the main parsing phases. Document helper parsing functions, utility methods, and common parsing patterns used across different language constructs. Explain parsing helpers for comments, preprocessor directives, and other language features that don't fit into major categories. Detail the integration of miscellaneous parsing utilities with the main parsing pipeline, shared parsing state management, and common error handling patterns. Include examples of utility function usage, parsing helper implementations, and their role in maintaining parsing consistency throughout the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Command Line Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the command-line interface tools. Document the main dls binary functionality including batch analysis modes, configuration options, and output formatting. Explain the DFA (Device File Analyzer) tool for standalone device analysis, dependency tracking, and report generation. Detail the MCP server command-line interface for AI-assisted development workflows, including server startup options, port configuration, and integration with AI assistants. Include practical examples of common CLI usage patterns, automation scripts, and integration with CI/CD pipelines. Address performance optimization for large-scale analysis, memory usage considerations, and debugging techniques for CLI operations. Document the relationship between CLI tools and the main language server, shared configuration options, and migration paths between different analysis modes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the dynamic configuration management system. Explain the configuration hierarchy from global settings to per-file overrides, LSP initialization options, and runtime configuration updates. Document the configuration file formats including compile_commands.json structure, lint configuration syntax, and DML-specific settings. Detail the configuration validation mechanisms, default value handling, and deprecated option migration strategies. Include practical examples of common configuration scenarios, troubleshooting configuration issues, and best practices for team-wide configuration management. Address the relationship between different configuration sources, precedence rules, and performance implications of complex configurations. Document the configuration API for programmatic access and integration with IDE settings.

wiki_catalogs.name: Actions and Request Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the action coordination and request handling system. Explain the action context management for maintaining server state across concurrent operations, request/response processing pipeline, and notification handling for real-time updates. Document the work pool coordination for managing parallel analysis tasks, progress reporting mechanisms, and resource allocation strategies. Detail the request queuing system, priority handling, and cancellation mechanisms for long-running operations. Include concrete examples of action lifecycle management, error propagation through the system, and performance optimization techniques. Address the relationship between actions and the broader server architecture, debugging approaches for concurrent operations, and scalability considerations for high-load scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing and Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the testing infrastructure and quality assurance practices. Explain the test architecture covering unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end testing strategies. Document the test suite organization including Rust-based tests, Python port tests, and cross-platform compatibility testing. Detail the testing frameworks used, mock strategies for external dependencies, and continuous integration setup. Include practical examples of writing new tests, debugging test failures, and performance regression testing. Address the testing of critical components like the analysis engine, LSP protocol compliance, and MCP functionality. Document the relationship between test coverage and code quality metrics, automated testing in CI/CD pipelines, and strategies for maintaining test reliability across different environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Python Port Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Python port alternative implementation. Explain the compatibility considerations between the Rust and Python versions, migration strategies for existing users, and development setup procedures. Document the Python-specific architecture choices, dependency management with pyproject.toml, and packaging considerations. Detail the feature parity status, performance characteristics compared to the Rust version, and use cases where the Python port is preferred. Include practical examples of Python port installation, configuration, and integration with Python-based development environments. Address the relationship between the two implementations, maintenance strategies, and contribution guidelines for the Python codebase. Document debugging techniques, profiling approaches, and troubleshooting common Python-specific issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development and Contributing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for development environment setup and contribution guidelines. Explain the development workflow including local build procedures, dependency management, and testing requirements. Document the code style conventions, architectural principles, and review processes for contributions. Detail the project governance, issue reporting procedures, and feature request handling. Include practical examples of common development tasks like adding new linting rules, extending analysis capabilities, and implementing LSP features. Address the relationship between different development environments (Rust vs Python), cross-platform considerations, and release procedures. Document the contribution licensing terms, code of conduct, and community engagement practices. Provide guidance for new contributors on finding suitable first issues and making meaningful contributions.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API reference documentation for all public interfaces and components. Document the main library exports, module-level APIs, and function signatures with detailed parameter descriptions and return value specifications. Include the LSP request/response schemas, MCP protocol specifications, and configuration API endpoints. Detail error codes, exception handling patterns, and diagnostic reporting mechanisms. Provide concrete examples of API usage from the actual codebase, parameter validation rules, and performance characteristics. Address backward compatibility guarantees, deprecation notices, and migration paths for API changes. Document the internal APIs exposed for extension development, plugin interfaces, and integration points for custom analysis tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Examples and Tutorials
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive examples and tutorial documentation demonstrating practical usage patterns and best practices. Include basic usage examples showing IDE integration, command-line analysis, and lint configuration setup. Document advanced configuration examples for complex DML projects, multi-device analysis scenarios, and custom linting rules. Provide integration examples with popular IDEs, build systems, and CI/CD pipelines. Include best practices for DML development workflows, performance optimization techniques, and troubleshooting common issues. Address real-world scenarios like migrating existing DML codebases, implementing custom analysis tools, and extending the language server functionality. Document the sample DML files, lint configuration examples, and practical templates for common development patterns.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# DML Language Server - Comprehensive Project Overview

## Project Introduction

The DML Language Server (DLS) is a sophisticated language server implementation designed specifically for Intel Simics Device Modeling Language (DML). This project provides comprehensive IDE support for DML device modeling, offering advanced static analysis capabilities and seamless integration with modern development environments.

### Purpose Statement
The DML Language Server serves as a background service that enhances developer productivity by providing real-time analysis, error detection, and intelligent code assistance for DML-based device modeling projects. It bridges the gap between complex hardware modeling requirements and modern software development workflows.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Advanced Static Analysis**: Provide comprehensive syntax and semantic analysis for DML 1.4 code
- **IDE Integration**: Support multiple IDEs and editors through the Language Server Protocol (LSP)
- **Code Intelligence**: Enable features like go-to-definition, find-references, hover information, and symbol navigation
- **Quality Assurance**: Implement configurable linting and style checking capabilities
- **AI-Assisted Development**: Offer MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration for intelligent code generation

### Target Audience
- Hardware engineers working with Intel Simics
- Software developers involved in device modeling
- IDE plugin developers seeking LSP integration
- Organizations requiring automated DML code analysis

## Technical Architecture

The DML Language Server follows a modular, event-driven architecture built on Rust's ownership system and concurrent programming primitives.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[DML Language Server] --> B[Language Server Protocol Handler]
    A --> C[VFS - Virtual File System]
    A --> D[Analysis Engine]
    A --> E[Lint Engine]
    A --> F[MCP Server]
    
    B --> G[Request Dispatcher]
    B --> H[Notification Handler]
    
    C --> I[File Operations]
    C --> J[Change Tracking]
    
    D --> K[Parsing Layer]
    D --> L[Semantic Analysis]
    D --> M[Symbol Resolution]
    
    E --> N[Style Checking]
    E --> O[Rule Application]
    
    F --> P[Tool Registry]
    F --> Q[Template Generation]
    
    G --> R[Go-to Definition]
    G --> S[Find References]
    G --> T[Hover Information]
    G --> U[Code Completion]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Server Components:**
- **Language Server Protocol Handler**: Manages LSP communication and request/response cycles
- **Virtual File System (VFS)**: Handles file operations, change tracking, and caching
- **Analysis Engine**: Performs syntax parsing, semantic analysis, and symbol resolution
- **Lint Engine**: Provides configurable code quality checking and style enforcement
- **MCP Server**: Enables AI-assisted development through model context protocols

**Design Patterns:**
- **Actor Model**: Event-driven processing with message passing between components
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable analysis strategies for different DML constructs
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time notifications for file changes and analysis updates
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic creation of analysis contexts and tool registries

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant IDE as IDE/Editor
    participant LSP as LSP Handler
    participant VFS as VFS Layer
    participant AE as Analysis Engine
    participant LE as Lint Engine
    
    IDE->>+LSP: Initialize Request
    LSP->>+VFS: Setup File Watchers
    LSP->>+AE: Parse Initial Files
    AE->>+LE: Apply Style Rules
    LE-->>-AE: Style Analysis Results
    AE-->>-LSP: Semantic Analysis
    LSP-->>-IDE: Server Capabilities
    
    IDE->>+LSP: Text Document Change
    LSP->>+VFS: Update File Cache
    LSP->>+AE: Incremental Analysis
    AE-->>-LSP: Updated Diagnostics
    LSP-->>-IDE: Publish Diagnostics
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Primary Server Entry Point:**
- `src/main.rs` - Main executable entry point with CLI argument parsing
- `python-port/dml_language_server/main.py` - Python implementation alternative

**Core Module Architecture:**
- `src/lib.rs` - Library entry point defining public API and module exports
- `src/server/mod.rs` - Central server orchestration and request handling
- `src/actions/mod.rs` - Action handlers for LSP requests and notifications

### Core Modules

**Analysis Pipeline:**
- `src/analysis/mod.rs` - Main analysis engine coordinating parsing and semantic analysis
- `src/analysis/parsing/` - Lexical analysis and syntax tree construction
- `src/analysis/structure/` - Semantic structure extraction and symbol table building
- `src/analysis/templating/` - Template instantiation and object hierarchy analysis

**Infrastructure Components:**
- `src/vfs/mod.rs` - Virtual file system with change tracking and caching
- `src/config.rs` - Configuration management with dynamic updates
- `src/lint/mod.rs` - Style checking and code quality enforcement
- `src/mcp/mod.rs` - Model Context Protocol server for AI-assisted development

### Configuration Approach

**Dynamic Configuration System:**
- `src/config.rs` - Runtime configuration with automatic inference and validation
- Supports both LSP initialization options and runtime configuration updates
- Handles deprecated option migration and unknown field detection

**Compile Commands Integration:**
- JSON-based configuration for DMLC compilation flags
- Automatic include path resolution for device imports
- Per-device compilation context management

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies:**
- `Cargo.toml` - Defines all Rust dependencies including LSP types, async runtime, and parsing libraries
- Key dependencies: `lsp-types`, `tokio`, `logos`, `rayon`, `serde_json`
- Cross-platform compatibility through `subprocess` and `walkdir`

**Python Port Dependencies:**
- `python-port/pyproject.toml` - Python ecosystem dependencies for alternative implementation
- Includes `pygls`, `lsprotocol`, `click`, and async file operations

### Integration Points

**Language Server Protocol:**
- Full LSP 3.0 specification compliance
- Support for all standard LSP features including diagnostics, hover, and go-to-definition
- Custom experimental features for advanced DML analysis

**MCP Integration:**
- Model Context Protocol for AI-assisted development
- Tool registry for code generation and analysis utilities
- Template-based object instantiation and validation

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class ActionContext {
        +init(options, capabilities, output)
        +inited() Result
        +pid() u32
    }
    
    class InitActionContext {
        +analysis: AnalysisStorage
        +vfs: Vfs
        +config: Config
        +client_capabilities: ClientCapabilities
    }
    
    class UninitActionContext {
        +analysis: AnalysisStorage
        +vfs: Vfs
        +config: Config
        +pid: u32
    }
    
    class AnalysisStorage {
        +isolated_analysis: HashMap
        +device_analysis: HashMap
        +linter_analysis: HashMap
    }
    
    ActionContext <|-- InitActionContext
    ActionContext <|-- UninitActionContext
    InitActionContext --> AnalysisStorage
    InitActionContext --> Vfs
    InitActionContext --> Config
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Core Language Features:**
- **Syntax Analysis**: Complete DML 1.4 syntax parsing with error recovery
- **Semantic Analysis**: Symbol resolution, type checking, and reference tracking
- **Navigation**: Go-to-definition, find-references, go-to-implementation
- **IntelliSense**: Hover information, symbol completion, and documentation

**Advanced Analysis:**
- **Device Context Analysis**: Multi-device analysis with dependency tracking
- **Template Processing**: Template instantiation and validation
- **Import Resolution**: Complex import hierarchies with circular dependency detection
- **Error Reporting**: Comprehensive diagnostic information with related locations

### Implementation Highlights

**Parsing Architecture:**
- `src/analysis/parsing/parser.rs` - Recursive descent parser with lookahead
- `src/analysis/parsing/lexer.rs` - Tokenization with DML-specific lexing rules
- `src/analysis/parsing/tree.rs` - Abstract syntax tree construction and manipulation

**Symbol Management:**
- `src/analysis/symbols.rs` - Comprehensive symbol table with scoping rules
- `src/analysis/reference.rs` - Reference resolution and cross-document linking
- `src/analysis/scope.rs` - Context-aware symbol resolution

**Style Checking:**
- `src/lint/rules/` - Modular rule system with configurable parameters
- `src/lint/README.md` - Extensive documentation for linting rules and configuration
- Support for per-file and per-line lint annotations

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Initializing: Initialize Request
    Initializing --> Ready: Server Capabilities
    Ready --> Analyzing: File Change/Save
    Analyzing --> Ready: Analysis Complete
    Ready --> Linting: Style Check Request
    Linting --> Ready: Lint Complete
    Ready --> MCP_Request: AI Tool Call
    MCP_Request --> Ready: Tool Response
    Ready --> Shutting_Down: Shutdown Request
    Shutting_Down --> [*]
```

**Analysis Pipeline Flow:**
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[File Content] --> B[Lexical Analysis]
    B --> C[Syntax Tree Construction]
    C --> D[Semantic Analysis]
    D --> E[Symbol Resolution]
    E --> F[Error Detection]
    F --> G[Diagnostic Generation]
    G --> H[Client Notification]
    
    I[User Input] --> J[Request Processing]
    J --> K[Analysis Trigger]
    K --> D
```

**MCP Integration Flow:**
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant AI as AI Assistant
    participant MCP as MCP Server
    participant DLS as DLS Core
    participant Templates as Template Engine
    
    AI->>+MCP: tools/call
    MCP->>+DLS: Process Tool Request
    DLS->>+Templates: Generate Code
    Templates-->>-DLS: Generated Templates
    DLS-->>-MCP: Tool Response
    MCP-->>-AI: Formatted Response
```

The DML Language Server represents a mature, production-ready solution for DML development environments, combining robust analysis capabilities with modern software engineering practices. Its modular architecture ensures maintainability and extensibility while providing comprehensive tooling support for hardware modeling workflows.

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [Cargo.toml](Cargo.toml)
- [src/lib.rs](src/lib.rs)
- [src/main.rs](src/main.rs)
- [src/config.rs](src/config.rs)
- [src/server/mod.rs](src/server/mod.rs)
- [src/analysis/mod.rs](src/analysis/mod.rs)
- [src/vfs/mod.rs](src/vfs/mod.rs)
- [src/lint/mod.rs](src/lint/mod.rs)
- [src/mcp/mod.rs](src/mcp/mod.rs)
- [src/mcp/server.rs](src/mcp/server.rs)
- [src/dfa/main.rs](src/dfa/main.rs)
- [python-port/README.md](python-port/README.md)

</blog>
